assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified test file has both additive changes (new dedup test, new assertions) and reductive changes (removed qualifier test); new test file is purely additive |'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence:
report.md lines 82-83 under 'Reductive signals': '-1 test function removed (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)
  - The removed test had 4 assertions (status OK, items.len == 2, two contains("repository_url="), items not equal)'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: The report's reductive signals section (lines 82-83) only lists the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers. The assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic is discussed under 'Additive signals' (line 79): 'Assertion specificity tightened: the basic test now asserts the exact simplified PURL string AND checks for absence of ?, whereas the base version only asserted the full qualified PURL string'. The report frames the changed assertion as 'tightened' (additive) rather than identifying the relaxation (going from checking a fully qualified PURL to a less-specific versioned PURL without qualifiers) as a reductive signal.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence:
report.md lines 89-90: '**New file: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`**
Purely additive: 3 new test functions with 12 new assertions covering edge cases (no-version PURL, mixed types, ordering preservation with pagination).'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 77 under 'Additive signals': '+1 test function added (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). All content is analytical/reporting in nature. No code files were created or modified. The report and criterion files contain only analysis, verdicts, and evidence — no code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and detailed findings but does not invoke any merge action. The report ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]...*' — it presents findings as a review comment for human consumption, not as an automated merge action.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification is produced by the Style/Conventions sub-agent (which spawns a test classification sub-agent for modified files) — the classification verdict is attributed to the Style/Conventions domain in the report
assertion_results.evidence: The report does not attribute the Test Change Classification to a 'Style/Conventions' domain or sub-agent. The classification appears as a standalone row in the main verification table (line 14) and as a standalone section 'Test Change Classification -- MIXED' (line 70). There is no mention of 'Style/Conventions sub-agent' or domain attribution anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: While the structural analysis does compare base-branch and PR-branch versions (line 74: 'Structural analysis (comparing base-branch and PR-branch versions)'), the semantic assessment explicitly references task requirements: line 87: 'The removal of test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers is intentional -- the task explicitly requires it ("Remove `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` (no longer applicable)")'. The semantic assessment references task requirements rather than being based solely on test file content, violating constraint 1.18.
